message.content:
You’re actually very close already — Telegram + model working is the hard part. What you’re missing is understanding how OpenClaw is structured internally.

Here’s the clean mental model + best resources (videos + guides) to get you unstuck.

🎥 Best “watch this first” tutorial

This is the most complete walkthrough right now. It specifically covers:

Memory system

Identity files

Workspace setup

Telegram + skills (you already did this)

Real-world usage

👉 If you only watch one thing, watch this.

📚 Good written guides (bookmark these)
🔗 Beginner quick start

OpenClaw Quick Start Guide

Super useful for verifying your setup is correct.

Key detail you probably missed:

You must enable the memory hook during onboarding 
Openclawd.wiki

🔗 Full beginner hub

OpenClaw Beginner Docs Hub

Covers install → channels → memory basics.

🔗 Troubleshooting + real-world setup

Complete Getting Started Tutorial

Good for fixing weird issues (gateway, onboarding, etc.)

🧠 The part you're missing: how OpenClaw actually works

OpenClaw is NOT configured in a dashboard.

It’s configured with files in your workspace.

Think of it like this:

🧱 The 3-layer system (this is the “aha” moment)
1. Identity (WHO the agent is)

These files define personality + purpose:

SOUL.md → personality + behavior

IDENTITY.md → name, vibe, emoji

USER.md → who you are

👉 If these are empty or generic → your agent feels “dumb”

2. Operations (HOW it behaves)

AGENTS.md → rules + instructions

HEARTBEAT.md → automation / periodic tasks

👉 This is basically your “system prompt on steroids”

3. Memory (WHAT it remembers)

memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md → daily logs

MEMORY.md → long-term important stuff

👉 Memory is just files, not a database 
TechRadar

⚠️ Why your setup feels incomplete

Based on what you said, you likely:

✅ Installed OpenClaw
✅ Connected Telegram
✅ Connected model

❌ Didn’t:

Define identity

Write meaningful AGENTS.md

Actually use memory files intentionally

Understand workspace-driven behavior

🛠️ How to fix your setup (simple path)
Step 1 — Open your workspace folder

Usually:

~/.openclaw/workspace/
Step 2 — Define your agent identity

Edit:

IDENTITY.md
Name: Atlas
Creature: AI operator
Vibe: calm, precise, slightly opinionated
Emoji: 🧭
SOUL.md

This is the BIG one. Example:

You are a proactive AI assistant.

You prioritize:
- Taking action over explaining
- Being concise but insightful
- Having opinions when useful

Avoid:
- Generic chatbot responses
- Over-explaining simple things
USER.md
Name: Ben
Timezone: (your timezone)
Notes:
- Interested in AI systems
- Wants automation and practical results
Step 3 — Add real memory

Create:

memory/2026-03-31.md

Put something like:

- User is setting up OpenClaw
- Has Telegram working but struggling with identity/memory
Step 4 — Upgrade AGENTS.md (this is huge)

This file controls behavior every time.

Add things like:

- Be proactive, not reactive
- Use memory files actively
- Suggest improvements to the system
- Prefer doing over explaining
Step 5 — Restart gateway
openclaw gateway restart
⚠️ Common mistakes (these trip everyone up)
1. “Why doesn’t it remember anything?”

Because:

Memory hook wasn’t enabled

OR memory files are empty

2. “It feels like ChatGPT, not an agent”

Because:

SOUL.md is weak or generic

AGENTS.md isn’t doing much

3. “It doesn’t improve over time”

Because:

You’re not writing to MEMORY.md

No structured memory process

🧠 Pro tip (this changes everything)

Just tell your agent:

“Set up your identity, memory system, and improve your workspace.”

OpenClaw is designed to self-configure via conversation — most people don’t realize that.

⚠️ Security note (important)

OpenClaw is powerful but risky if misconfigured:

Don’t expose it publicly

Limit permissions

Use a VPS or sandbox if possible 
TechRadar

If you want, I can help you directly

If you paste:

your current SOUL.md

AGENTS.md

I’ll rewrite them into something actually powerful (this is where most setups fail).
